title: Biliary Disease
objectives: Understand the pathophysiology of Biliary Disease
Identify key diagnostic criteria for Biliary Disease
Recognize complications and when to escalate care

sections.title: Overview & Pathophysiology
sections.content: **Biliary Disease** — This section covers the fundamental pathophysiology, epidemiology, and clinical significance. Understanding the underlying mechanisms is essential for clinical reasoning and evidence-based management.

sections.title: Clinical Presentation & Diagnosis
sections.content: Key clinical features, diagnostic criteria, and workup for Biliary Disease. This section emphasizes pattern recognition and systematic diagnostic approaches.

sections.title: Management & Treatment
sections.content: Evidence-based management strategies for Biliary Disease, including first-line therapies, escalation pathways, and monitoring parameters.

sections.title: Complications & Follow-Up
sections.content: Potential complications, prognosis, and longitudinal management considerations for Biliary Disease.

keyTakeaways: Recognize the classic presentation of Biliary Disease
Identify red flags requiring urgent intervention

reviewQuestions.question: Which of the following is the MOST important initial step in evaluating a patient with suspected Biliary Disease?
options: Obtain a thorough history and physical examination
Consult a specialist before any evaluation
reviewQuestions.explanation: A thorough history and physical examination is always the cornerstone of clinical evaluation for Biliary Disease, guiding subsequent diagnostic workup and management decisions.

reviewQuestions.question: What is the MOST critical factor in determining the management approach for Biliary Disease?
reviewQuestions.explanation: Clinical severity and appropriate risk stratification guide management decisions in Biliary Disease, determining the level of intervention and monitoring required.

reviewQuestions.question: Which complication of Biliary Disease requires the MOST urgent intervention?
reviewQuestions.explanation: Hemodynamic instability in the setting of Biliary Disease requires immediate intervention including fluid resuscitation, close monitoring, and potentially escalation to critical care.

imaging_reasoning.order_reasoning: US is first-line (fast, no radiation); HIDA if US equivocal (cystic duct obstruction)
imaging_reasoning.expected_findings: Cholelithiasis, gallbladder wall thickening, pericholecystic fluid, CBD dilation if cholangitis
imaging_reasoning.management_impact: Cholecystitis → cholecystectomy; cholangitis → ERCP for decompression; Tokyo criteria guide severity
